help_dialog_report: To report any problem, please contact us:
messagebox_screenshot_with_adjustment: The current media has adjustments. Do you want to take a screenshot without the adjustments ?
messagebox_global_screenshot_error: A problem occurred during the screenshot process.
dialog_save_project_dialog_text: The timeline contains unsaved changes. Do you want to save the project?
messagebox_record_completed: The media record was completed successfully.
open_more_than_four_files_dialog: The media player is limited to 4 simultaneous media files. Do you want to load your selection into the playlist?
project_exportation_finished: The media export was completed successfully.
messagebox_format_not_accepted: One of the selected files has an unsupported format.
messagebox_fullscreen_experimental: The software's fullscreen feature is currently under development.
help_menu_open_files_content_1: At startup, the player displays a blank screen. To open a media file, click on the "Files" menu and then "Open media files" to open a file selection window. You can also use the play button located in the toolbar at the bottom of the screen.
help_menu_open_files_content_2: It is also possible to open media files by performing a drag and drop of the desired file onto the black area of the player.
help_menu_classic_toolbar_content_1: A toolbar is displayed below the media it is associated with. It allows you to perform various operations on the media.
help_menu_play_pause_description: Allows you to play or pause the media.
help_menu_stop_description: Resets the timer of the media to 0 and pauses it.
help_menu_eject_description: Removes the media from the player.
help_menu_change_media_description: Switches to the previous/next media in the playlist order.
help_menu_speed_description: Opens a slider to modify the playback speed of the media. The supported speeds are x0.25, x0.5, x0.75, x1.0, x1.25, x1.5, x2.0
help_menu_loop_description: Toggle media loop playback on or off.
help_menu_volume_description: Opens a slider to adjust the media volume.
help_menu_language_description: Allows selecting audio and subtitle languages for the media.
help_menu_media_info_description: Shows technical information and metadata for the media.
help_menu_zoom_description: Toggle zoom mode. With zoom active use the mouse wheel to zoom and Ctrl (Cmd on Mac) + left click to pan.
help_menu_screenshot_description: Capture a screenshot of the current media frame.
help_menu_extraction_description: Opens a dialog to enter start and end timecodes to extract the corresponding sequence.
help_menu_duplicate_description: Allows duplicating the selected media into a new player. Changes on the duplicate only apply to it.
help_menu_fullscreen_description: Toggle fullscreen mode on or off.
help_menu_extended_toolbar_buttons_descr: Description of the extended toolbar buttons
help_menu_hide_media_description: Show a black screen instead of the media. Hovering the button reveals a slider to set opacity.
help_menu_adjustments_description: Shows various sliders to set brightness, contrast, saturation and hue of the media.
help_menu_draw_description: When draw mode is active you can draw over the media and pick stroke parameters.
help_menu_compo_rule_description: Displays various composition rules over the media.
help_menu_backward_description: Allows skipping backward or forward 5 seconds in the media.
help_menu_prev_frame_description: Step to the previous or next frame of the media.
help_menu_record_description: Allows recording the currently playing media; time jumps are considered.
help_menu_rotate_description: Rotate the media 90° to the right. ⚠️ If the media is paused the screen remains black. Restart playback to see the transform.
help_menu_flip_description: Flip the media horizontally or vertically. ⚠️ If the media is paused the screen remains black. Restart playback to see the transform.
help_menu_enter_multiview_content_1: It is possible to display 1 to 4 players simultaneously. In multi-view mode (when 2 or more players are present), each media has its own simplified toolbar and a global bar appears at the bottom for operations affecting all medias.
help_menu_enter_multiview_content_2: To enter multi-view mode, hover the media layout button at the top-right. You can then choose the number of players and their layout. Players are resizable by dragging the splitters between them. You can also use the duplicate button.
help_menu_simplified_content_1: The simplified toolbar available in multi-view players includes most buttons from the classic toolbar but without extensions. There is also a button to remove the player from the view.
help_menu_global_toolbar_content_1: In addition to per-player simplified toolbars, a global toolbar is shown in multi-view to perform operations on the medias present.
help_menu_global_toolbar_content_2: Combined screenshot captures an image of each present media and combines them into a single image preserving the players layout. Screenshots are saved to the folder set in preferences (default NumalysePlayer_Content).
help_menu_access_playlist_content_1: A playlist can be created by adding the desired medias. To access the playlist click the side panel button at the top-right (playlist is not available in multi-view mode).
help_menu_manage_items_content_1: Playlist items consist of the media title, its duration, type, thumbnail and position in the list. Click + to add media and the trash icon to remove them. Items can also be reordered with drag & drop.
help_menu_playlist_loop_description: If enabled, moves to the next media when the current one ends.
help_menu_playlist_shuffle_description: If enabled, medias are played in random order.
help_menu_playlist_sort_description: Sort the list according to the selected criteria.
help_menu_playlist_delete_description: Remove all items from the playlist. A confirmation will be shown.
help_menu_timeline_display_content_1: The detailed timeline provides a view of audio (purple bar) and the different video tracks (blue bar). Tracks are annotatable and can be added manually or automatically. Segmented videos can be saved as projects and exported in various formats.
help_menu_timeline_display_content_2: The timeline shows a time scale, a blue video view with the track's initial image, a purple audio view with the waveform and buttons to edit tracks. By default there is a single track. You can zoom in on the timeline using the scroll wheel, select a clip by left-clicking (Ctrl+left-click to select multiple clips), and move horizontally using Shift+left-click. Additional options are available by right-clicking on the clips.
help_menu_ab_loop_description: Click: place an A/B marker at the current position. Once both markers are set the video loops on that range; clicking again removes the markers.
help_menu_segmentation_description: Automatically detect transitions in the video and apply the result to the timeline. Previous segmentation will be overwritten.
help_menu_split_description: Split the track at the current position into two tracks.
help_menu_merge_description: Merge the current track with the previous/next one.
help_menu_change_shot_description: Set the timecode to the beginning of the previous/next track.
help_menu_shot_detail_description: Open the track information in the side panel. Title and notes are editable.
help_menu_export_description: Open a dialog to choose the project export format.
help_menu_shot_infos_content_1: Current track informations are viewable with the side panel button at the top-right or via the corresponding button in the timeline. It shows numbering, title, start, end and duration as timecodes, notes and the reference image. Title and notes fields are editable.
help_menu_exportation_content_1: The current project can be saved via the 'Files' → 'Save' menu. Projects can be exported as text (.txt and .docx), PDF, PowerPoint, as annotated video (titles and notes per track) and as reference images (all reference images are saved into the configured folder).
project_manager_error_json_file_not_foun: The project save file could not be found.
project_manager_error_cannot_open_json_f: Cannot open the project. The file might be locked or damaged.
project_manager_error_json_parsing_error: The project file is corrupted and cannot be read.
project_manager_error_media_file_not_fou: The media associated with this project could not be found.
project_manager_error_media_key_missing: The project save file is damaged: media information is missing.
project_manager_error_media_mismatch_fps: The current media is different from the project (the frame rate is different).
project_manager_error_media_mismatch_dur: The current media is different from the project (the duration has changed).
project_error_copy_failed: Failed to copy the file. Please try again or check your disk space.
